  "findings": [
    "Mechanical seal face contaminated by bare-hand touch prior to installation."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the contaminated seal. Do not install.",
    "Obtain a new, factory-clean mechanical seal.",
    "Review clean handling procedures with the technician: wear clean, lint-free gloves; handle only by the outer diameter or designated grip areas; keep seal faces covered until the moment of installation."
  ],
  "rationale": "Mechanical seal faces require absolute cleanliness. Skin oils, salts, and microscopic debris from bare-hand contact create a contamination film that prevents proper face flatness and lubrication, leading to immediate leakage, overheating, and premature failure upon startup. SKF/Timken mounting guides and standard seal OEM practices mandate handling seal faces only with clean gloves and protecting them from any contamination. Installing a contaminated seal is a high-risk non-compliance that will cause a reliability incident.",
  "references": [
    "SKF/Timken mounting guides (clean handling of precision sealing surfaces)",
    "OEM mechanical seal installation instructions (no bare-hand contact with seal faces)"
  ]
}
